title: Johnson & Johnson — Immunology — Latest Briefing
html:
J&J Immunology Quarterly Briefing — Q1 2026

Quarter: January–March 2026 Earnings reported: J&J April 14 | AbbVie April 25 | Novartis April 22 | BMS April 24 | UCB April 30 (pending) | Lilly April 30 (pending) | Amgen April 29 (pending) Generated: 2026-04-27

Governing Thought

TREMFYA hit $1.6B in Q1 (74% growth, beat estimates by $400M) and became the IBD new-start share leader — but the full competitive picture reveals a market where AbbVie's Skyrizi+Rinvoq are collectively generating $7B+ per quarter in the same indications. ICOTYDE launched March 18 into a psoriasis market where UCB's Bimzelx is the fastest-growing injectable and BMS's Sotyktu is proving oral biologics can win. The math problem is no longer whether TREMFYA can grow — it's whether J&J can capture enough of the IL-23/immunology expansion before AbbVie's two-product flywheel locks in treatment algorithms.

1. J&J Financial Signals

Metric Q1 2026 Commentary
J&J total sales $24.1B 9.9% reported growth; raised 2026 outlook to $100-101B
TREMFYA quarterly $1.6B Beat $1.2B estimate — massive upside surprise
TREMFYA growth +74% YoY Fastest-growing IL-23 therapy in the US
TREMFYA IBD position New-start share leader First time officially leading in IBD new starts
Immunology segment -8.8% YoY Stelara biosimilar erosion overwhelming TREMFYA gains
ICOTYDE launch March 18, 2026 "Strong demand" from day one
2026 outlook raise +$300M Driven by ICOTYDE + new product contributions
  • $1.6B vs. $1.2B estimate is a 33% earnings beat — signals that the street was underestimating GI uptake
  • "New-start share leader in IBD" is the competitive milestone TREMFYA needed vs. Skyrizi's data advantage
  • Immunology -8.8% shows the Stelara hole is enormous — TREMFYA needs to grow >50% annually just to hold segment revenue flat

2. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
AbbVie Skyrizi ~$4.8B +52% IBD driving acceleration; "best-in-class" framing intensifying
AbbVie Rinvoq ~$2.3B +51% UC/CD label expansion; JAK class concerns fading
AbbVie Humira ~$1.4B -41% Biosimilar erosion; AbbVie explicitly shifting promotional spend to Skyrizi/Rinvoq
Novartis Cosentyx ~$1.4B +7% Mature but stable; IV formulation in PsA adding incremental growth
BMS Sotyktu ~$160M +85% Small base but strong trajectory; oral TYK2 gaining traction in psoriasis
BMS Zeposia ~$170M +22% Modest UC contribution; not a major competitive force
UCB Bimzelx Pending (Apr 30) ~+70% est. HS launch fueling growth; fastest-growing injectable biologic in derm
Lilly Taltz Pending (Apr 30) Flat est. Stable but deprioritized in Lilly's portfolio (GLP-1 dominance)
Amgen Otezla Pending (Apr 29) -5% est. Declining as biologics take oral share; ICOTYDE could accelerate erosion
TREMFYA $1.6B +74% Context: TREMFYA is #3 in immunology revenue behind Skyrizi and Cosentyx

AbbVie — Skyrizi ($4.8B) + Rinvoq ($2.3B) + Humira ($1.4B)

AbbVie Q1 2026 call (April 25, 2026):

AbbVie's immunology commentary is the most important competitive signal this quarter. Key themes:

  1. Skyrizi in IBD — AbbVie claims 75% capture rate among IL-23 new starts: Management stated that among patients starting an IL-23 in frontline IBD, Skyrizi captures ~75% vs. TREMFYA's ~25%. AbbVie frames this as "class leadership" and points to SEQUENCE head-to-head data (Skyrizi superiority vs. Stelara in CD) as the driver. However, J&J's claim of "new-start share leader in IBD" includes all new starts (not just IL-23 class), suggesting TREMFYA is winning against non-IL-23 competitors even as it trails Skyrizi within-class.

  2. Combined Skyrizi+Rinvoq guidance: $27B for FY2026 ($34.5B by 2028): AbbVie is projecting the two-product immunology franchise at nearly the size of J&J's entire Innovative Medicine division. This dual-brand approach — Skyrizi (biologic) + Rinvoq (oral JAK) — gives AbbVie a tool for every patient profile. J&J's TREMFYA + ICOTYDE is the direct counter-strategy, but ICOTYDE launched only 6 weeks before quarter-end.

  3. Humira transition explicitly completed: AbbVie described the Humira-to-Skyrizi/Rinvoq transition as "essentially complete in derm and rheum." Promotional resources have fully shifted. This means Skyrizi is now receiving 100% of AbbVie's immunology promotional intensity — TREMFYA faces the full force.

  4. AbbVie on GI specifically: Acknowledged IL-23 class is growing rapidly in UC/CD but framed it as "class expansion benefits everyone" rather than a zero-sum TREMFYA vs. Skyrizi fight. Management singled out Rinvoq's rapid-response profile in UC as a differentiator vs. biologics (faster onset than both IL-23s).

Implication for J&J: The 75% vs. 25% IL-23 capture in IBD is the number Vinoth's team needs to interrogate. Is it real, or is AbbVie measuring in a way that flatters Skyrizi? ZoomRx's GI PET data can validate or challenge this claim. If true, TREMFYA's "new-start share leadership" is coming from stealing TNF/JAK patients, not from winning the IL-23 within-class battle.

UCB — Bimzelx (~€380M estimated, pending Apr 30)

UCB Q1 2026 earnings pending (April 30), but extrapolating from H2 2025 trajectory:

  1. Fastest-growing injectable in derm: Bimzelx has been consistently cited as the fastest-growing biologic in dermatology, with dual IL-17A/F inhibition resonating with dermatologists who want superior efficacy over IL-23. UCB's 2025 annual report showed Bimzelx approaching €1.3B.

  2. HS approval (Dec 2024) is the growth catalyst: Hidradenitis suppurativa approval gives Bimzelx a second high-growth indication. HS is largely unpenetrated by biologics — AbbVie's Humira was the only prior option, and biosimilar erosion creates an opening.

  3. Not a GI competitor — but a derm one: Bimzelx lacks GI approvals (IL-17 inhibitors generally don't work in IBD). This means Bimzelx competes with TREMFYA in psoriasis/PsA/AxSpA but not in UC/CD. ICOTYDE is more directly in Bimzelx's crosshairs in derm.

Implication for J&J: In dermatology, the battle is IL-23 (TREMFYA/Skyrizi) vs. IL-17 (Bimzelx/Cosentyx/Taltz) vs. oral (ICOTYDE/Sotyktu/Otezla). Bimzelx's rapid growth suggests dermatologists are NOT uniformly converging on IL-23 — efficacy arguments matter, and Bimzelx's PASI 100 rates are hard to beat.

Novartis — Cosentyx (~$1.4B, +7%)

Novartis Q1 2026 call (April 22, 2026):

  1. Cosentyx is the IL-17 incumbent: ~$1.4B quarterly puts Cosentyx at ~$5.5B annual run rate. Growth has slowed to single digits (vs. 15%+ in prior years), reflecting maturity and Bimzelx/Taltz competition.

  2. IV formulation in PsA/AxSpA is the play: Novartis launched IV Cosentyx as a differentiation move — faster onset and loading dose advantage. Not a TREMFYA competitor in GI, but relevant in rheumatology where IL-17 and IL-23 overlap.

  3. HS expansion pursuing: Novartis is running Cosentyx in HS, where it now competes against Bimzelx and potentially TREMFYA (J&J has explored IL-23 in HS-adjacent conditions).

Implication for J&J: Cosentyx's slowdown in derm is an opportunity. Patients stepping off Cosentyx (or choosing beyond it) increasingly go to IL-23 or the newer IL-17s. TREMFYA should be positioned as the "step-up" for Cosentyx partial responders.

BMS — Sotyktu ($160M, +85%) + Zeposia ($170M, +22%)

BMS Q1 2026 call (April 24, 2026):

  1. Sotyktu is proving oral biologics can work in psoriasis: At $160M quarterly (small base), Sotyktu is the only FDA-approved oral biologic for psoriasis besides Otezla. +85% growth validates the oral convenience thesis. BMS reported that Sotyktu's new-to-brand share is growing, particularly among patients who refuse injectables.

  2. BMS framing Sotyktu vs. ICOTYDE directly: BMS management acknowledged ICOTYDE's approval but emphasized Sotyktu's "proven real-world track record" and TYK2's "differentiated safety profile" vs. IL-23 receptor antagonism. This is the first direct competitive positioning against ICOTYDE from a rival.

  3. Zeposia in UC is modest: ~$170M quarterly in UC/MS combined. Not a material competitive force against TREMFYA in GI, but signals that S1P modulators are gaining a toehold in the UC algorithm.

Implication for J&J: ICOTYDE's biggest near-term competitor for "needle-phobic" patients isn't Skyrizi — it's Sotyktu. The ICOTYDE vs. Sotyktu messaging battle (IL-23 oral vs. TYK2 oral) will play out at dermatology conferences and in field force encounters. ZoomRx should track this head-to-head positioning.

Amgen — Otezla (~$550M est., pending Apr 29)

Amgen Q1 2026 earnings pending, but Otezla trajectory is well-established:

  1. Declining as biologics dominate: Otezla peaked at ~$2.8B annually in 2023 and has been declining 3-5% annually as dermatologists shift moderate patients directly to biologics.

  2. ICOTYDE is the existential threat: ICOTYDE offers oral convenience (Otezla's advantage) plus IL-23-class efficacy (far superior to PDE4 inhibition). If ICOTYDE captures even 20% of Otezla's base, that's ~$100M/year redirected to J&J.

  3. Amgen deprioritizing Otezla: Amgen's promotional focus is on Tezspire (asthma) and oncology biosimilars. Otezla is in managed decline.

Implication for J&J: ICOTYDE should be positioned directly against Otezla in the "oral psoriasis" category. The efficacy gap is enormous (55% PASI 90 for ICOTYDE vs. ~20% for Otezla), and the convenience parity removes Otezla's only remaining moat.

Eli Lilly — Taltz (~$750M est., pending Apr 30)

Lilly Q1 2026 earnings pending, but Taltz is a known quantity:

  1. Stable but deprioritized: Taltz generates ~$3B annually but Lilly's entire promotional apparatus is focused on Mounjaro/Zepbound (GLP-1). Taltz receives minimal incremental investment.

  2. IL-17A monotherapy losing ground: As Bimzelx (dual IL-17A/F) gains, single IL-17A agents like Taltz face within-class competition. In psoriasis, Taltz is increasingly the "mature" IL-17.

  3. Not a factor in GI: Like all IL-17 agents, Taltz has no GI indications. The TREMFYA competitive overlap is limited to derm and rheum.

Implication for J&J: Taltz attrition is an opportunity pool. Dermatologists switching patients off Taltz are choosing between Skyrizi, TREMFYA, and Bimzelx. ZoomRx PET data should capture this switching dynamic.

Synthesis: What the Combined Picture Tells Us

  1. The immunology market is ~$45B+ annually and still growing 15-20% — TREMFYA's 74% growth sounds dominant but AbbVie alone generates 3x TREMFYA's quarterly revenue from the same indications. J&J is winning in the fastest-growing segment (GI new starts) while AbbVie owns the installed base.

  2. AbbVie's 75% IL-23 capture in IBD is THE competitive metric to validate — if accurate, TREMFYA's "new-start share leadership" is coming from displacing TNF and JAK, not from beating Skyrizi within IL-23. That's still good news (market expansion > share steal) but the Skyrizi battle is far from won.

  3. The oral revolution is real — ICOTYDE, Sotyktu, and Otezla combined represent a ~$3-4B oral psoriasis market. BMS is already positioning against ICOTYDE. J&J needs to win the oral category decisively — which means ICOTYDE messaging must lead with efficacy superiority over both Otezla AND Sotyktu.

  4. IL-17 is not dead — Bimzelx's growth shows dermatologists still value IL-17 for superior skin clearance. The IL-23 vs. IL-17 debate is not settled, despite IL-23 winning the narrative. Multi-domain patients (skin + joints + gut) go to IL-23; skin-only patients may still prefer IL-17 for speed and depth.

3. Clinical/Regulatory

  • ICOTYDE FDA approval (March 18, 2026): First oral IL-23 receptor antagonist for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis (adults + adolescents ≥12 years, ≥40 kg)
  • ICOTYDE 1-year data: Lasting skin clearance with favorable safety through 52 weeks; adverse events within 1.1% of placebo
  • TREMFYA IBD real-world evidence: Post-marketing data strengthening; contributing to new-start share leadership
  • TL1A pipeline: Multiple Phase 2-3 programs advancing in IBD (Roche, Merck, others) — long-term class threat to IL-23 agents
  • Skyrizi CD superiority data (SEQUENCE): AbbVie continuing to leverage head-to-head superiority vs. Stelara in CD — TREMFYA lacks a direct head-to-head vs. Skyrizi

4. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Evidence This Quarter
1. TREMFYA can lead in IBD new starts CONFIRMED J&J claims new-start share leadership — but AbbVie claims 75% IL-23 capture; ZoomRx must validate
2. ICOTYDE disrupts the oral category EARLY SIGNAL — POSITIVE "Strong demand" from day one; BMS already positioning Sotyktu against it; Otezla vulnerable
3. AbbVie's two-product flywheel is the real threat STRENGTHENED $7.1B combined Q1 from Skyrizi+Rinvoq vs. $1.6B TREMFYA; the gap is structural, not cyclical
4. Stelara erosion defines immunology math CONFIRMED -8.8% segment decline; Humira transition at AbbVie "essentially complete" — J&J's transition is not
5. IL-17 class remains competitive in derm CONFIRMED Bimzelx fastest-growing injectable; Cosentyx stable at $5.5B; IL-17 vs IL-23 debate persists

5. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q2 2026)

  1. AbbVie Skyrizi Q2 revenue — does it sustain 50%+ growth? If yes, the $27B FY2026 guidance is achievable and J&J's relative position weakens
  2. ICOTYDE Q2 revenue (first full quarter) — $50M+ would be a strong launch; the Sotyktu comparison is the benchmark (~$85M in its first full quarter)
  3. UCB Bimzelx Q1 actual (April 30) — if Bimzelx crosses €400M quarterly, it's a €1.6B run-rate product and a material competitor in derm
  4. ZoomRx GI PET data — can it validate or challenge AbbVie's 75% IL-23 capture claim? This is the highest-value data point in all of immunology
  5. ICOTYDE vs. Sotyktu early prescribing patterns — who are dermatologists choosing for the "oral biologic" patient? First-mover data is critical
  6. TL1A readouts — any Phase 3 data at DDW 2026 could reshape the IBD competitive landscape

6. Conversation Starters for J&J

  1. "AbbVie says Skyrizi captures 75% of IL-23 new starts in IBD. You say TREMFYA is the new-start share leader. Both can be true if you're winning against TNF/JAK while losing within IL-23. Our GI PET data can tell you exactly which — and that answer determines whether your promotional strategy should be class-expansion or head-to-head." — Directly addresses the most important competitive ambiguity.

  2. "BMS is already positioning Sotyktu against ICOTYDE on your Q1 earnings call. The oral biologic category is going to be defined in the next 6 months — not by efficacy data, but by dermatologist perception of convenience vs. mechanism vs. safety. We should be tracking ICOTYDE messaging effectiveness against Sotyktu specifically, not just generically." — Surfaces a competitive threat most clients won't see coming.

  3. "AbbVie's Skyrizi+Rinvoq are generating $7B per quarter. Your immunology segment declined 8.8%. The Stelara hole is bigger than TREMFYA's growth. ICOTYDE is the bridge — but the real question is whether you can get to $10B TREMFYA peak before AbbVie locks in treatment algorithms. That's a timing question, and we can measure algorithm stickiness in real-time." — Frames the strategic challenge in terms J&J leadership thinks about.

Sources: J&J Q1 2026 earnings call (Apr 14), AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings call (Apr 25), Novartis Q1 2026 earnings call (Apr 22), BMS Q1 2026 earnings call (Apr 24). UCB, Lilly, and Amgen Q1 2026 pending (Apr 29-30). Revenue estimates for pending companies based on trailing trends.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

3. Clinical/Regulatory

ICOTYDE FDA approval (March 18, 2026): First oral IL-23 receptor antagonist for moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis (adults + adolescents ≥12 years, ≥40 kg)
ICOTYDE 1-year data: Lasting skin clearance with favorable safety through 52 weeks; adverse events within 1.1% of placebo
TREMFYA IBD real-world evidence: Post-marketing data strengthening; contributing to new-start share leadership
TL1A pipeline: Multiple Phase 2-3 programs advancing in IBD (Roche, Merck, others) — long-term class threat to IL-23 agents
Skyrizi CD superiority data (SEQUENCE): AbbVie continuing to leverage head-to-head superiority vs. Stelara in CD — TREMFYA lacks a direct head-to-head vs. Skyrizi

5. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q2 2026)

AbbVie Skyrizi Q2 revenue — does it sustain 50%+ growth? If yes, the $27B FY2026 guidance is achievable and J&J's relative position weakens
ICOTYDE Q2 revenue (first full quarter) — $50M+ would be a strong launch; the Sotyktu comparison is the benchmark (~$85M in its first full quarter)
UCB Bimzelx Q1 actual (April 30) — if Bimzelx crosses €400M quarterly, it's a €1.6B run-rate product and a material competitor in derm
ZoomRx GI PET data — can it validate or challenge AbbVie's 75% IL-23 capture claim? This is the highest-value data point in all of immunology
ICOTYDE vs. Sotyktu early prescribing patterns — who are dermatologists choosing for the "oral biologic" patient? First-mover data is critical
TL1A readouts — any Phase 3 data at DDW 2026 could reshape the IBD competitive landscape
